This document includes instructions on how to prepare stock solutions
for icPDC media, OmGel, dissociation enzymes, and dead cell marker (DCM)
used in flow cytometry.

# Stock solutions for icPDC media

**0.1% BSA**

To reconstitute certain reagents, a 0.1% BSA solution is required. Note
that the amount of 15 mg is based on the limitations of the available
scale, which cannot accurately weigh less than 10 mg.

Dissolve 15 mg in 15 mL DPBS (without Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺); the orbital shaker
in the cold room can be used to aid dissolution. Sterile filter with a
0.22 µm strainer before use.

**B-27 supplement Cat no. 17504044 ThermoFischer**

Thaw the 50x stock vial (10 mL) for at least 2 hours in the fridge.

Aliquot 10x 1 mL per Eppendorf. Store at -20°C.

Working solution: 1x: add 2 mL of 50x stock in 100 mL medium

**β-estradiol Cat no. E2758-1G   Sigma/Merk**

Prepare stock solution 10mM: Dissolve 0.027 g of the powder in 10 mL of
absolute alcohol.

Aliquot 10x 100 µL and 9x 1 mL stock solution per Eppendorf. Store at
-80°C.

Working 100 nM: dilute 1 uL of stock in 100 mL medium

**Animal-Free Recombinant Human EGF   Cat no. AF-100-15-100ug
Peprotech **

Prepare stock solution 100 µg/mL: Dissolve 100 µg in 1 mL sterile 0.1%
BSA in DPBS (without Ca & Mg). Do not filter it.

Aliquot into 25x 40 μL per sterile tube. Store at -80°C.

Working solution 5 ng/mL: Add 5 μL of 100 μg/mL in 100 mL of medium

**Animal-Free Recombinant Human FGF-basic (154 a.a.) Cat no. AF-100-18B
Peprotech**

Prepare stock solution 100 µg/mL: Dissolve 50 µg (supplied) in 500 µL
sterile 0.1% BSA in DPBS (without Ca & Mg). Do not filter it.

Aliquot into 10x 50 µL per tube. Store at -80°C.

Working solution 10 ng/mL: Add 10ul of 100ug/mL in 100 mL of medium

epare stock solution 100 µg/mL: Dissolve 50 µg (supplied) in 500 µL
sterile 0.1% BSA in DPBS (without Ca & Mg). Do not filter it.

Aliquot into 10x 50 µL per tube. Store at -80°C.

Working solution 10 ng/mL: Add 10ul of 100ug/mL in 100 mL of medium

**Recombinant human FGF-10 Cat no.100-26          Peprotech**

Prepare stock solution 100 µg/mL: Dissolve 100 µg in 1 mL sterile 0.1%
BSA in DPBS (without Ca & Mg). Do not filter it.

Aliquot into 20x 50 µL per tube. Store at -80°C.

Working solution 10 ng/mL: Add 10ul of 100ug/mL in 100 mL of medium

**Recombinant Human Heregulin β-1 Cat no. 100-03 Peprotech**

Prepare stock solution 50 µg/mL (6.6 µM): Dissolve 100 µg of the powder
in 2 mL of sterile 0.1% BSA in DPBS (without Ca and Mg).

Aliquot 11 x 180 µL stock solution per tube. Store at -80°C.

Working solution 5 nM:  dilute 76uL of stock in 100 mL medium

**Hydrocortisone Cat no. H0888 Sigma/Merk**

Prepare stock solution 1 mg/mL: Dissolve 0.015 g of the powder in 15 mL
of absolute alcohol.

Aliquot 10x 120µL and 13x 1mL stock solution per Eppendorf. Store at
-80°C.

Working solution 500 ng/mL:  dilute 50uL of 1mg/mL stock in 100 mL
medium

**Recombinant Human IL-2 200-02 1 mg Peprotech**

Prepare stock solution 1mg/mL or 6.6 x10\^7 units/mL: Centrifuge the
vial, and dissolve in 1 mL 0.1% BSA in DPBS (without Ca and Mg).

Aliquot 40x 25µL per tube. Store at -80°C.

Working solution 500 IU/mL: 0,75 ul of stock solution on 100 mL of
medium.

**Recombinant Human IL-7 Cat no: 200-07 10 µg Peprotech**

Prepare 100 µg/mL stock in sterile MQ water: dissolve 10 µg in 100 ul of
sterile MQ water.

From this prepare working stock 10 µg/mL: add 900 ul of sterile 0.1% BSA
to the 100 µL of 100ug/mL.

Aliquot into 18 x 55µL per sterile tube. Store at -80°C.

repare 100 µg/mL stock in sterile MQ water: dissolve 10 µg in 100 ul of
sterile MQ water.

From this prepare working stock 10 µg/mL: add 900 ul of sterile 0.1% BSA
to the 100 µL of 100ug/mL.

Aliquot into 18 x 55µL per sterile tube. Store at -80°C.

Working concentration 10 ng/mL: add 10 μL of 10 μg/mL working stock to
10 mL of organoid medium.

**Recombinant Human IL-15  Cat no: 200-15 10 µg Peprotech**

Prepare 100 µg/mL stock in sterile MQ water: dissolve 10 µg in 100 µL of
sterile MQ water.

From this prepare working stock 10 µg/mL: add 900 µL of sterile 0.1% BSA
to the 100 µL of 100 µg/mL.

Aliquot into 18 x 55µL per sterile tube. Store at -80°C.

Working concentration 10 ng/mL: add 10 μL of 10 μg/mL working stock to
10 mL of medium

**Y-27632 / ROCK inhibitor Cat no. M1817 Abmole bioscience**

Prepare stock solution 5 mM: Dissolve 10mg (whole bottle) of the powder
in 8.086 mL DMSO.

No need to filter this.

Aliquot 16 x 500 µL per tube. Store at -80°C.

Working solution 5uM: Add 100ul of 5mM in 100 mL of medium

# Stock solution for OmGel 

**Aprotinin Cat no. A1153-10mg Sigma-Aldrich**

3-8 TIU/mg solid, lyophilized powder

To prepare a 10 mg/mL stock solution: dilute 10 mg in 1 mL mQ-water.

Aliquot 33x 30 µl per tube. Store at -20°C.

NOTE! The original iPDC paper has a mistake with the aprotinin catalog
number; instead of A6279 by Sigma, A1153 was used.

**Fibrinogen Cat no. 341578-500mg Calbiochem/Sigma-Aldrich**

To prepare a 25 mg/mL stock solution: Add 10 mL of warm sterile mQ-water
to the bottle with 500 mg of fibrinogen. Mix slowly with the pipette to
avoid bubbles. Meanwhile, keep the water in the water bath. Then add
another 10 mL of warm sterile mQ-water and mix that carefully.

Aliquot 30 x 600 µL per tube. Store at -80°C.

the bottle with 500 mg of fibrinogen. Mix slowly with the pipette to
avoid bubbles. Meanwhile, keep the water in the water bath. Then add
another 10 mL of warm sterile mQ-water and mix that carefully.

Aliquot 30 x 600 µL per tube. Store at -80°C.

NOTE! The original iPDC paper has a mistake in the fibrinogen
concentration used. The concentration was documented to be 25 µg/mL, and
then the working concentration was 0.5 µg/ml. In reality, the stock
solution was 25 mg/mL, and the working concentration should be 0.5 mg/mL
(as it is written in the Neilson et al., doi:
[10.1016/j.mbplus.2023.100136](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.mbplus.2023.100136)).
However, the ratio is the same, so all iPDC cultures have been prepared
with the final fibrinogen concentration of 0.5 mg/mL.

**Thrombin Cat no. T6884-100UN Sigma-Aldrich**

lyophilized powder, [≥]{.mark} 2,000 NIH units/mg protein

To prepare a 100 U/mL stock solution: Dilute 100 units in 1 mL of 0.1%
BSA in sterile PBS (or DPBS).

Aliquot into 20 x 50 µL per tube. Store at -80°C.

# Dissociation enzymes

**Dispase** **Cat no. D4693-1g** **Sigma-Aldrich**

Use sterile filtered (0.22 µm) 10 mM NaAc (sodium acetate, pH 7.5) to
dilute the dispase.

To prepare a 50 U/mL stock solution: Transfer the dispase powder to a
clean and sterile 100 mL glass bottle. Add 10 mL of 10 mM NaAc to the
bottle; use some of the NaAc to rinse the dispase stock bottle. Shake
gently for approximately 15 minutes using the shaker in the +4°C cold
room until the powder is dissolved.

Aliquot into 20x 500 µL per tube. Store at +4°C.

**Collagenase & Hyaluronidase Cat no. 07912 STEMCELL Technologies**

Thaw the stock vial overnight at +40°C.

Aliquot into 8x 1 mL and 4x 500 µL per tube. Store at -20°C.

# DCM for flow cytometry

Aliquot into 20x 500 µL per tube. Store at +4°C.

**Collagenase & Hyaluronidase Cat no. 07912 STEMCELL Technologies**

Thaw the stock vial overnight at +40°C.

Aliquot into 8x 1 mL and 4x 500 µL per tube. Store at -20°C.

# DCM for flow cytometry

**LIVE/DEAD™ Fixable Aqua Dead Cell Stain, for 450 nm excitation Cat no.
L34956 Invitrogen**

Thaw the aqua fluorescent reactive dye and DMSO vials on ice for
\~15min. Centrifuge the dye vial, and add 50 µL of DMSO on top. Mix
carefully.

Aliquot 6x 8 µL aliquots per tube. Store at -20°C.

 7. Choose C y t o m e t e r P e r f o r m a n c e from the drop down menu. Check you have

 8. When CST has passed

 Short trouble-shoo ng for failed CST (by Mar n Ivarsson, Karolinska Instutet) 
 CST sometimes does not pass. Don’t be alarmed, usually it’s simple to ix. 
 If FSC/SSC or other plots looked weird (eg. All events on the axis) you might not have 
 the right ilters in place. Check them and replace standard ilters (see sheet on the 
 paper tray above desk). 
 You should have about 200-600 events/second when running CST. If the event rate is 
 too low or high, adjust the low rate or the CST bead concentration accordingly. 
 You may also clean the low cell from air bubbles and debris: 
 1. Abort CST (keep the tube of CST beads) 
 2. Load a tube with H20 
 3. Prime 3x on the instrument front panel (any air bubbles in the 
 low cell will be purged). 
 4. Load a tube of FACS Flow and run HIGH for 5 min (re ills the low 
 cell with liquid). 
 5. Load the CST bead tube again, set the low rate to LOW or 
 MEDIUM and restart CST. 
 6. Repeat if the problem persists. Also check the air ilter outside the 
 instrument as well as sheath and waste levels. 
 Other tips: 
 1. Make new CST beads with fresh FACS Flow or PBS 
 2.

in, set the low rate to LOW or 
 MEDIUM and restart CST. 
 6. Repeat if the problem persists. Also check the air ilter outside the 
 instrument as well as sheath and waste levels. 
 Other tips: 
 1. Make new CST beads with fresh FACS Flow or PBS 
 2. Clogging might be the reason: Run FACS Clean at HIGH low rate for 5 min and 
 then run H2O for 5 min. 
 3. Another tip from BD if CST keeps failing nothing else seems to work is to load a 
 tube with few mL of H2O and do the following: 
 ● Press Prime for 3 seconds. 
 ● Press Run for 3 seconds interrupting the priming. 
 ● Press Prime for 3 seconds. 
 ● Repeat 20-30 times. 
 According to BD, this will create a lot of turbulence in the low cell and remove any 
 “micro-bubbles ”. 
 3

 1. Load a tube with 2-3mL FACS Clean and run it on HIGH low rate for 1 min the

# Dissociation protocol for icPDC cultures

**Materials:**

Laminar hood

Cell culture centrifuge (cool down to +4[°C)]{.mark}

37[°C]{.mark} rotating incubator

Sterile surgical tools and a scalpel

10 cm tissue culture plates (#664160, Greiner)

70 µm cell strainers (#22-363-548, Fisher)

50 mL Falcon tubes

15 mL Falcon tube

1.5 mL/ 2 mL Eppendorf tubes

DMEM F12 (#11320-074, Thermo Scientific): Cell culture fridge or cold
room

GlutaMAX ™, 100X (#35050-61, GibcoLife Technologies): Cell culture
cupboard RT

Penicillin-Streptomycin, 10000 U/mL (#15140122, GibcoLife Technologies):
Lola's room freezer

HEPES (#H0887, Sigma): Cell culture fridge

Dispase II, 50 U/mL(#D4693-1G, Sigma): Färkkilä lab fridge lower drawer

Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H), 10x (#07912, STEMCELL Technologies):
wet lab freezer Enzymes box)

RQ1-RNAse free DNAse (#M610A, Promega): Färkkilä lab -20[°C]{.mark}
enzyme box

RBC lysis buffer (#11814389001, Roche): cell culture fridge

Stem cell banker (#11890F, Amsbio): cell culture fridge

Cryovials (#122263, Greiner)

Cell freezing / Mr. Frosty box

For tissue dissociation, the following media is used:

DMEM/F12+++: DMEM/F12 supplemented with 1x Glutamax (5 mL of 100x) + 10
mM HEPES (5 mL of 1M) + 1 % Penstrep (5 mL).

To prepare, remove 15 mL of DMEM/F12 from the media bottle and add 5 mL
of each supplement. Label the bottle with the added components, date,
and your initials. The ready-to-use medium is stored in the cell culture
fridge.

Note! DMEM/F12 is different from Advanced DMEM/F12.

1. **Preparation before sample collection**

 1. Bring ice.

 2. Switch on the 37[°C]{.mark} incubator (located in Färkkilä lab
 > below the lab bench)

 3. Prepare the laminar hood as follows: Clean the surface with 70%

ent from Advanced DMEM/F12.

1. **Preparation before sample collection**

 1. Bring ice.

 2. Switch on the 37[°C]{.mark} incubator (located in Färkkilä lab
 > below the lab bench)

 3. Prepare the laminar hood as follows: Clean the surface with 70%
 > Ethanol, and stick the plastic bag to the right-hand side
 > glass wall for collecting waste tissues and tips. Place the
 > sterile forceps, scissors, scalpel, and 10 cm tissue culture
 > plate inside the laminar hood.

 4. Prepare Virkon: Add 2 tablets of Virkon into a 1 L bottle. Fill
 > up the bottle with 1 L of tap water. Write the date and your
 > initials, and let it dissolve.

Before starting the dissociation:

- Remove the patient ID sticker

- If the sample is large or the entire tumor piece is not needed, 1)
 > ask if Anastasia wants to biobank an additional tissue piece,
 > and 2) ask if the Kauppi lab is interested in taking a piece of
 > the tumor.

During the dissociations:

- If you plan to proceed with icPDC cultures using the dissociated
 > cells, prepare the icPDC media during the incubation steps.
 > Ideally, the media should be ready by the time the
 > dispase-dissociated cells are resuspended in fresh media following
 > RBC lysis and centrifugation.

**Dissociation protocol:**

1. **Sample preparation:**

 1. Transfer the tissue into a 10 cm plate. If the tissue is from
 > the omentum, remove the normal-looking omentum surrounding the
 > tumor tissue. If the sample is from the ovary/adnexa, remove
 > the capsule surrounding "normal" tissue.

 2. Mince the tumor piece into smaller

2. **Enzymatic dissociation using 1) dispase and 2) collagenase &
 > hyaluronidase (C&H) mix:**

 1. Prepare **dispase** mix with 9.700 mL DMEM/F12+++, 200 µL of
 > Dispase stock, and 100 µL RNAse-free DNase I. Increase the

tumor piece into smaller

2. **Enzymatic dissociation using 1) dispase and 2) collagenase &
 > hyaluronidase (C&H) mix:**

 1. Prepare **dispase** mix with 9.700 mL DMEM/F12+++, 200 µL of
 > Dispase stock, and 100 µL RNAse-free DNase I. Increase the
 > volume depending on the tissue size.

 2. Transfer the minced tissue into a 50 mL Falcon tube containing
 > the dispase mix.

 3. Seal the lid with parafilm, and vortex briefly (3x 3 sec.).

 4. Incubate inside the 37°C rotating incubator for 30 min with
 > rotation set at 13.

 5. Prepare the **C&H mix** with 8.9 mL DMEM/F12+++ media, 1 mL C&H
 > (10x stock), and 100 µl RQ1 RNase-Free DNase. Increase the
 > volume depending on the tissue size.

 6. To obtain a single-cell suspension after the dispase
 > dissociation, filter the samples with a 70 µm cell strainer.

 1. Pre-wet the filter with 1 mL DMEM/F12+++, transfer the
 > digested suspension through the filter into a 50 mL Falcon
 > tube, and wash the stainer with 5 mL media.

 2. If the strainer gets clogged, mix the tissue homogenate on
 > top of the filter with a 1 mL tip.

 3. **Keep the tissue leftovers.**

 4. Centrifuge the obtained cell suspension for 10 min at 1000
 > rpm 4[°C]{.mark} (cell culture centrifuge). To finish the
 > processing of both cell suspensions together, resuspend
 > the cells in icPDC media. Keep the cells on ice until
 > centrifuged again at step 2.10.3. From there onwards,
 > continue the process with the sequentially dissociated
 > cells.

 7. Transfer the leftover tissue pieces into a 50 mL Falcon tube
 > containing the **C&H mix**.

 8. Seal the lid with parafilm, and vortex briefly (3x 3 sec.).

 9. Incubate inside the 37[°C]{.mark} rotating incubator for 1 hour
 > with rotation set at 13.

 10.

the leftover tissue pieces into a 50 mL Falcon tube
 > containing the **C&H mix**.

 8. Seal the lid with parafilm, and vortex briefly (3x 3 sec.).

 9. Incubate inside the 37[°C]{.mark} rotating incubator for 1 hour
 > with rotation set at 13.

 10. To obtain single-cell suspension from C&H, filter the samples
 > with a 70 µm cell strainer.

 1. Pre-wet the filter with 1 mL DMEM/F12+++, transfer the
 > digested suspension through the filter into a 50 mL Falcon
 > tube, and wash the stainer with 5 mL media.

 2. If the strainer gets clogged, mix the tissue homogenate on
 > top of the filter with a 1 mL tip.

 3. Centrifuge the obtained cell suspension for 10 min at 1000
 > rpm 4[°C]{.mark} (cell culture centrifuge). If dispase
 > dissociated cells are not yet treated with RBC lysis,
 > centrifuge them also. Alternatively, for sequentially
 > dissociated cells, centrifugation can be increased to 300
 > g for 5 min.

3. **Red blood cell (RBC) lysis**

 1. Upon centrifugation, aspirate the supernatant and resuspend the
 > cell pellet in 2 mL- 5 mL RBC lysis buffer, depending on the
 > pellet size.

 2. Incubate at RT for 5 min.

 3. Add 10 mL (for 2 mL lysis buffer) - 20 mL (for 5 mL lysis
 > buffer) cold DMEM/F12+++ medium, and mix well.

 4. Centrifuge 10 minutes at 1000 rpm 4^o^C (cell culture
 > centrifuge).

4. **Resuspension of the cell pellets**

 1. Carefully remove the supernatant.

 2. Resuspend the pellet of dispase-dissociated cells in icPDC media

 3. And resuspend the pellet of sequentially dissociated cells in
 > DMEM/F12+++ medium.

 4. Decide the volume of resuspension depending on the pellet size.
 > If the pellet is large resuspension volume can be up to 5 mL.

5. **Viability analysis using Trypan Blue:**

 1.

ellet of sequentially dissociated cells in
 > DMEM/F12+++ medium.

 4. Decide the volume of resuspension depending on the pellet size.
 > If the pellet is large resuspension volume can be up to 5 mL.

5. **Viability analysis using Trypan Blue:**

 1. If the pellet is huge, resuspending it in 5 mL may not be
 > diluted enough for counting. Therefore, a 1:10 dilution can be
 > made: mix 10 µL of thoroughly mixed cell suspension with 90 µL
 > of DMEM/F12+++.

 2. Pipette 10 µL of the cell suspension into a 1.5 mL tube and add
 > 10 µL of trypan blue. Mix thoroughly.

 3. Pipette 10 µL onto each side of the LUNA cell-counting chamber
 > (2 sides total).

 4. Use Bright Field Cell Counting, DEFAULT protocol with Trypan
 > Blue (1:1) as the staining option.

 5. Record the total cell density (cells/mL), live cell density
 > (cells/mL), viability (%), and cell size (µm) from both sides.
 > Calculate the average and total cell count. Remember to take
 > into account the total volume and whether you prepared a
 > dilution.

6. **Cleaning**

 1. Place a Virkon tablet in the sample jar with excess tissue and
 > HBSS.

 2. Put the used scalpel, scissors, and tweezers into an empty tip
 > box containing premade Virkon solution.

 3. Pour Virkon solution onto the used 10 cm plate.

# Enrichment protocol to isolate CAFs 

This protocol concerns only the sequentially dissociated cells.
Meanwhile, keep the dispase-dissociated cells on ice.

Magnetic-activated cell sorting (MACS) is used with a negative selection
strategy to enrich CAFs from the sequentially dissociated cell
suspension.

Negative isolation: Depleting the sample from cancer and immune cells
using a mixture of anti-EpCAM and anti-CD45 (TIL) beads, and an LS
column in a QuadroMACS separator.

**Materials:**

Laminar hood

Negative isolation: Depleting the sample from cancer and immune cells
using a mixture of anti-EpCAM and anti-CD45 (TIL) beads, and an LS

**Materials:**

Laminar hood

anti-CD45 (TIL) beads (#130-118-780, Miltenyi): cell culture fridge

LS column in a QuadroMACS separator (Miltenyi)

Prepare the MACS buffer in advance. Combine 49.55 mL of DPBS (without
MgCl2 and CaCl2), 250 µL of FBS, and 200 µL of EDTA to achieve a final
concentration of 0.5% FBS and 2 mM EDTA in DPBS. Sterile-filter the
buffer using a 0.22 µm filter, then allow it to degas, as air bubbles
can block the column.

1. Preparations

 1. Based on the cell count, divide the cell suspension into equal
 > fractions so that the maximum number of cells does not exceed
 > the recommended sample size.

 1. Recommended sample size for leukocytes: 0.1 E6 - 100 E6
 > labeled cells in 10 E6 - 2000 E6 total cells per column.

 2. Calculate the volume of the MACS buffer, and both anti-EpCAM and
 > anti-CD45 beads

 1. Volume of both beads is 20 µL per 10 E6 cells.

 2. The volume of the buffer is 500 µl - the volume of both

2. Magnetic labeling

 1. Resuspend the cell pellet in the required volume of MACS buffer.

 2. Add the required volumes of MicroBeads.

 3. Mix well and incubate 30 min at +4 °C shielded from light.

 4. Wash the cells by adding 1 mL buffer per 10 E6 cells, and

 5. Aspirate the supernatant.

 6. Resuspend up to 100 E6 cells in 500 µL of buffer.

3. Magnetic separations

 3. Apply the cell suspension onto the column through the filter.

 4. Wash the column with 3 x 3mL of MACS buffer. Collect unlabeled

# Combining dispase-dissociated cells with enriched CAFs

1. Calculate the required volume of the combined cell suspension.

 1. Consider the volumes needed for flow cytometry aliquots and for
 > cell plating.

 2. For flow cytometry, aliquots containing 0.8 E6 -- 1.5 E6 cells
 > are suitable.

2. Determine the necessary volumes of each suspension based on the
 > ratio of dispase-dissociated cells to flow-through cells (enriched
 > CAFs).

 1. Calculate using the live cell counts.

3. Add the calculated volumes of both cell suspensions together.

4. Mix the suspension thoroughly.

5. Calculate the concentration of the resulting combined cell
 > suspension.

# Biobanking

1. Depending on the cell yield, biobank cells from the obtained cell
 > suspensions.

 1. At a minimum, biobank cells from the dispase-dissociated cell
 > fraction.

2. Aim to biobank at least 3 vials. Each vial can contain between 0.5 -
 > 20 E6 cells.

3. Resuspend the cells to be biobanked in DMEM/F12+++, and centrifuge
 > for 10 minutes at 1000 rpm +4 °C.

4. Aspirate the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 1 mL of
 > Stem Cell Banker. Add the additional volume of Stem Cell Banker to
 > reach 1 mL per cryovial.

5. Label each cryovial with the following information: sample ID,
 > dissociation method, cell count, date, and initials.

6. Place the tubes in a cell-freezing box and transfer to −80 °C.

7. The following day, move the cryovials to the patient sample cell

**Project introduction:** This project aims to elucidate how
cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) in high-grade serous ovarian cancer
(HGSC) influence responses to emerging anti-cancer therapies. To achieve
this, we will develop a novel 3D ex vivo model integrating
patient-derived HGSC tumor cells, tumor-resident immune cells, and CAFs
to more accurately recapitulate the complexity of the tumor
microenvironment. Using this model, we will then perform functional
drug-response assays to identify CAF-mediated mechanisms of therapeutic
resistance and uncover potential strategies to improve treatment
efficacy.

**Project lead:** María Hincapié-Otero, MSc (Doctoral researcher, 2023 -
present)

**Project members:** María Hincapié-Otero, Aino Elomaa, Sarah Wolf, Nika
Mikhailava, Kürsat Birgin, Sara Palomino, Foteini Chamchougia

**External collaborators:** Dr. Heidi Haikala, Bassel Alsaed (Haikalab),
Zac Darroch (Haikalab), Lilja Lahtinen (Haikalab)
